label: Class · Interactive Spaces
subtitle: Projection mapping, camera-based touch, voice-controlled surfaces
verdict: Lecture-heavy. Solid lineage from PixelSense → Xperia → today; homography section needed a second pass on the math. Pre-filled from slide deck — no transcript captured.

sections.label: The big idea · surfaces as interfaces
sections.summary: A projector + a camera + software turns any surface into an interface. No screens, no bezels — the interface dissolves into the room.

sections.label: Precedents · who's done this?
sections.summary: Microsoft PixelSense (2007), Sony Xperia Touch (2017), Disney's Living Surfaces, Lightform. The dream is older than the hype.

sections.label: Approach · our stack
sections.summary: Browser + projector + webcam. MediaPipe for hands and faces. AprilTags for objects. WebSockets for state. Zero-install and live-reload.

sections.label: Math · homography
sections.summary: Four corner points + a 3×3 matrix maps a tilted projector image onto a flat surface. Worked through the math live; class needed two passes.

sections.label: Architecture · two windows, one channel
sections.summary: Editor window edits, projector window projects, BroadcastChannel keeps them in sync. One canvas, two views.

sections.label: Tools · public entry points
sections.summary: Live demo of the public Surface Studio entry point + the archived prototypes index — a graveyard of one-off ideas worth raiding.

sections.label: Widgets + actions
sections.summary: Walked through the widget palette and the TouchOSC-style action graph. Class wanted to build immediately — saved for the next session.

sections.label: Hand + face tracking
sections.summary: MediaPipe in the browser — 30 fps, 21 hand keypoints, 478 face keypoints. Live demo on the projector worked perfectly.

sections.label: Voice + LLM
sections.summary: Web Speech + Ollama for room voice commands. Showed “make it red” → action wired. Latency made it feel less magical than it should.

sections.label: Live walkthrough + try it
sections.summary: Quick exercise: each pair sketched a one-widget surface that would solve a real classroom moment.

assignments.title: Pick a precedent, write 200 words
assignments.spec: Pick one historical interactive-spaces project (PixelSense, Xperia Touch, Living Surfaces, etc.). Write 200 words on what it got right and what killed it.
rubric: Names the project with year and team.
Identifies what worked and what didn't — with evidence.
Connects to one of our class's own affordance themes.

assignments.title: Homography walkthrough
assignments.spec: Re-do the homography math on paper. Map four projector-corner points onto four surface corners. Submit a photo of the worked solution.
assignments.ambiguity: If you've done this in another class, just attach a one-sentence note saying so — no need to redo it.

assignments.title: Sketch a one-widget surface
assignments.spec: One widget, one surface, one classroom moment it solves. Hand-drawn or digital, doesn't matter.
rubric: Widget purpose clear in one sentence.
Names which sensing channel feeds it (hand / face / voice / tag).

grammar.issue: Homography matrix derivation lost the back of the room.
grammar.fix: Pre-render the worked example as a slide; walk through the answer rather than the derivation.

ideas.title: Whiteboard-as-surface
ideas.summary: Project annotations onto the whiteboard and let the marker actually become an input device. Camera sees the stroke, projection responds.

ideas.title: Door-frame greeter
ideas.summary: Project soft welcome onto the wall by the door when the room recognises someone arriving — recognised because their tag is in the doorway.

ideas.title: Light-mood by sound
ideas.summary: Web Speech listens for room volume; projector dims when class quiets down.

engagement.label: Demos run live
engagement.note: All five planned demos ran without falling back to slides.

engagement.label: Hands raised on math
engagement.note: Homography section had visibly fewer questions than usual.

engagement.label: Sketch ideas fielded
engagement.note: Twelve viable one-widget ideas surfaced in the closing exercise.

actions.title: Re-author the homography slide as a worked example
Push demo links to Slack — public-entry index
Pre-warm Ollama on lab Mac before the next session

quotes.speaker: Mark Weiser
quotes.text: The most profound technologies are those that disappear. They weave themselves into the fabric of everyday life.

quotes.speaker: Brett
quotes.text: A projector turns any surface into an interface. The trick is the camera that watches what people do with it.

quotes.speaker: Brett
quotes.text: We're building connections and experiences between people. The technology is the seam.
